content.full_text:
'.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 5, 1990

Honorable Frank Hill
Member of the Assembly
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Senate District 31 Election
Our File No. A-89-707

Dear Assemblyman Hill:

We have received your amended Candidate Intention (Form 501)
and Campaign Bank Account (Form 502) notices in connection with
the special election for Senate District 31.

For your information, as both an elected state officer and a
candidate for Senate District 31, you will incur campaign
disclosure obligations during 1990 under both Sections 84200.7 and
84200.8 of the Political Reform Act.¹ Section 84200.7 requires
the filing of pre-election campaign statements in connection with
the June 5, 1990 state primary election. Section 84200.8 requires
the filing of pre-election campaign statements in connection with
the February 6 and April 10, 1990 special and special run-off
elections. However, some of these statements can be combined
pursuant to Section 84205, which states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports required
to be filed by this title.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Honorable Frank Hill
Page 2


Therefore, you should file campaign statements through
June 30, 1990, using the enclosed schedule.2 In addition, please
refer to the 1989 FPPC "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure
Provisions of the Political Reform Act" for detailed information
regarding campaign disclosure. (For example, the second pre-
election statement filed in connection with each election must be
sent by guaranteed overnight mail or delivered in person.) The
1990 Information Manual will be available in February.

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact
me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Carla J. Wardlow
Carla J. Wardlow
Assistant Chief, Technical
Assistance & Analysis Division

Enclosures




2
The Commission has advised that whenever a candidate or
officeholder is required to file campaign statements, all of his
or her controlled committees (other than a committee formed
primarily to qualify, support or oppose a state ballot measure)
must file campaign statements at the same times and in the same
places as the candidate. (Barrett Advice Letter, No. A-88-150/88-
168, copy enclosed.)

Honorable Frank Hill
Page 3

FEBRUARY 6 AND APRIL 10 ELECTION FILING SCHEDULE
FOR ELECTED STATE OFFICERS AND OFFICEHOLDERS
WHO WILL ALSO BE VOTED UPON JUNE 5, 1990


FOR CANDIDATES BEING VOTED UPON ON FEBRUARY 6, 1990:

Type of Statement          Period Covered           Filing Deadline

1st Pre-Election           Jan. 1¹ - Dec. 23       Dec. 28, 1989

2nd Pre-Election           Dec. 24 - Jan. 20       Jan. 25, 1990

Late Contribution          Jan. 21 - Feb. 5        Within 24 Hours
Reporting Period

_____________________________________________________________________

FOR CANDIDATES ALSO BEING VOTED UPON IN THE APRIL 10 RUN-OFF:

1st Pre-Election           Jan. 21 - Feb. 24       March 1, 1990

2nd Pre-Election           Feb. 25 - March 24      March 29, 1990

Late Contribution          March 25 - April 9      Within 24 Hours
Reporting Period

2nd Pre-Election (for      March 25 - May 19       May 24, 1990
June election)

_____________________________________________________________________

FOR CANDIDATES WHO LOSE THE FEBRUARY 6 ELECTION:

1st Pre-Election (for      Jan. 21 - March 17      March 22, 1990
June election)

2nd Pre-Election (for      March 18 - May 19       May 24, 1990
June election)

Late Contribution          May 20 - June 4         Within 24 Hours
Reporting Period

_____________________________________________________________________

FOR ALL CANDIDATES:

Semi-Annual                The day after the       July 31, 1990
                          closing date of the
                          last statement filed -
                          June 30

_____________________________________________________________________

¹     The period covered by any campaign statement begins on the
day after the closing date of the last statement filed or
January 1 if no previous statement has been filed.

sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations for an elected state officer who is simultaneously a candidate in multiple special elections (February 6 and April 10 special elections) and the June 5, 1990 primary election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The candidate must file pre-election campaign statements under both Sections 84200.7 and 84200.8, but may combine certain statements pursuant to Section 84205. The FPPC provides a detailed filing schedule through June 30, 1990, specifying different filing deadlines and periods covered for candidates based on election outcomes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' headers typical of advice letters, but is clearly an FPPC response letter providing specific filing guidance. Content extracted from body text. File number A-89-707 with January 5, 1990 date. Includes detailed filing schedule table as enclosure reference.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations for an elected state officer who is simultaneously a candidate in multiple special elections (February 6 and April 10 special elections) and the June 5, 1990 primary election?

CONCLUSION: The candidate must file pre-election campaign statements under both Sections 84200.7 and 84200.8, but may combine certain statements pursuant to Section 84205. The FPPC provides a detailed filing schedule through June 30, 1990, specifying different filing deadlines and periods covered for candidates based on election outcomes.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises Assemblyman Frank Hill on campaign disclosure obligations as both an elected state officer and candidate for Senate District 31, providing a detailed filing schedule for multiple overlapping elections in 1990.
